overview: Temur Control in Standard stabilizes through resource acceleration and graveyard recursion, leveraging Stock Up for early mana and Consult the Star Charts for flexible draw. The deck aims to outlast opponents by cycling spells into the graveyard and reactivating them via Flashback. Victory comes from maintaining board control while building a reusable hand of value-generating cards.
win_conditions: Drawing into answers with Consult the Star Charts
Outvaluing opponents through Stock Up acceleration

keep_criteria: Keep hands with 2-3 lands (prioritizing fixing/duals), at least one early interaction (counterspell, removal, or discard), and a draw engine (Stock Up or a flashback enabler).
mulligan_criteria: Mulligan on 3+ lands with no early plays, or hands with only late-game threats and zero disruption.

early_game: Cast Stock Up to ramp and draw
Begin cycling cards for Flashback fuel
Turn 1: Play a land, optionally cast a cheap cantrip or early interaction if available.
Turn 2: Develop your mana base, deploy a counterspell or removal to handle early threats, or cast Stock Up/flashback spells to stabilize and draw into answers.
Prioritize fixing over rushing lands to ensure consistency.
mid_game: Use Consult the Star Charts to find specific answers
Hoard mana for key counterspells and removal until the opponent commits.
Use flashback strategically to recycle high-impact spells without clogging your hand.
Manage graveyard resources carefully to enable flashback while avoiding hate.
Commit threats only when you have a clear path to resolution or when the board is stabilized.
Hold back on overcommitting to one spell
always keep a counter or removal for the opponent's biggest play.
late_game: Control the board with repeated removal
Deploy resilient mid-to-late game threats (e.g., planeswalkers, flying creatures, or value engines) that are hard to interact with.
Use flashback to recur removal or counters to protect your finisher.
Go all-in when you’ve established board control, have card advantage, and the opponent has no clear interaction left.
Also commit when you can safely cast a finisher through their remaining mana.

key_cards.name: Consult the Star Charts
key_cards.notes: Draw into answers when ahead, or cycle to fuel Flashback when behind.

key_cards.name: Stock Up
key_cards.notes: Play early to accelerate mana and draw cards, enabling faster access to flashback spells.

key_cards.name: Flashback
key_cards.notes: Prioritize cycling or discarding spells with this ability to reuse them for board control or value.

sequencing_tips: Cycle low-value cards early to fuel Flashback
Hold up interaction before casting Stock Up
Use Consult the Star Charts to dig for specific answers
Prioritize Flashback on high-impact spells over minor ones
don’t over-rely on it early if it leaves your graveyard vulnerable to hate.
Consult the Star Charts and Stock Up provide card advantage, but sequence them carefully to avoid flooding.
Against the Izzet/landfall meta, prioritize interaction over card draw in the early game.

matchups.advice: Focus on early interaction and graveyard setup.
Race with flashback recursion and card advantage.
Stabilize with Consult the Star Charts and outvalue them.
Use flashback to cast spells from the graveyard, bypassing counterspells. Employ cheap cantrips to force counters and find answers. Use uncounterable threats or split your spells across turns.
Prioritize early interaction to slow their mana development. Use sweepers and artifact/enchantment removal to neutralize their acceleration. Out-value them with card draw and efficient removal.

Win the resource war by out-drawing and out-interacting. Prioritize card advantage and efficient counterspells. Use flashback to recycle key spells and maintain pressure. Track their graveyard to anticipate their flashback threats.

common_threats: Watch for mana bases and land drops to predict spell availability.
Track graveyard contents to anticipate flashback threats.
Note when opponents hold up mana for multiple turns—they’re likely setting up a combo or big spell.
